knowledgeGuidance.title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. The EBR should include the customer's executive sponsor and your internal VP of Customer Success. Agenda: review ROI achieved, address top concerns, present renewal roadmap. Prepare a slide deck with usage metrics, outcomes delivered, and a 12-month value roadmap.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Expansion and Upsell Opportunity Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: Identify expansion opportunities when: (1) customer adoption is above 70%, (2) they have expressed interest in additional modules, (3) their team has grown. Steps: (1) Prepare a usage report showing value delivered, (2) Present product roadmap with upcoming features, (3) Propose expansion SKUs aligned with their pain points, (4) Involve your AE for commercial negotiation. Target: expand ARR by 20%+ on renewal.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Proactive Renewal Management Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: Initiate renewal conversations 90 days before the renewal date. If auto-renewal is disabled, treat this as a high-priority renewal risk. Steps: (1) Review contract value and discount eligibility, (2) Schedule renewal discovery call with economic buyer, (3) Prepare a Business Value Assessment showing ROI over the contract period, (4) Present renewal proposal with multi-year incentive if applicable.

scenarios.name: Executive Business Review
scenarios.summary: Bring executives together to validate the renewal plan and address churn risk.

scenarios.name: Quarterly Payment Plan
scenarios.summary: Offer a flexible payment schedule to balance cash flow and retention.

scenarios.name: 20% Discount
scenarios.summary: Use a price incentive to improve renewal likelihood while accepting margin pressure.

scenarios.name: Additional Product Training
scenarios.summary: Improve adoption through targeted training to increase engagement and retention.

scenarios.name: No Action
scenarios.summary: Maintain the status quo and do not intervene aggressively.

bestScenario.name: 20% Discount
bestScenario.summary: Use a price incentive to improve renewal likelihood while accepting margin pressure.

potentialWeaknesses: Potential revenue impact from the selected option.
The selected scenario may create customer expectations that are hard to sustain.
longTermDisadvantages: Margin pressure could harm future renewal negotiations.
It may reduce ability to pursue higher-value upsell opportunities.
revenueTradeOffs: Short-term renewal may come at the cost of long-term revenue growth.
customerRelationshipRisks: Customers may expect similar concessions in the next cycle.

alternative.name: Quarterly Payment Plan
alternative.summary: A balanced approach that preserves revenue while improving renewal probability.

devilsAdvocateReview.finalVerdict: The selected scenario should be reviewed for risk; a more balanced alternative may be preferable.

knowledgeGuidance.action: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. knowledgeGuidance.title: Expansion and Upsell Opportunity Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: Identify expansion opportunities when: (1) customer adoption is above 70%, (2) they have expressed interest in additional modules, (3) their team has grown. Steps: (1) Prepare a usage report showing value delivered, (2) Present product roadmap with upcoming features, (3) Propose expansion SKUs aligned with their pain points, (4) Involve your AE for commercial negotiation. knowledgeGuidance.title: Low Adoption Intervention Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If product adoption falls below 50%, schedule an Adoption Workshop within 14 days. Identify the top 3 unused features relevant to the customer's use case. Assign a Solutions Engineer to run a hands-on training session. Follow up with a 30-day adoption improvement plan and weekly check-ins. Measure adoption weekly post-workshop.

domainDetection.reasoning: The documents consistently highlight themes central to Customer Success, including customer renewal dates, adoption metrics (NPS, CSAT), onboarding issues, executive sponsor engagement in quarterly reviews, customer escalations due to dissatisfaction, and potential expansion opportunities linked to customer success and product adoption.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. The EBR should include the customer's executive sponsor and your internal VP of Customer Success. Agenda: review ROI achieved, address top concerns, present renewal roadmap. Prepare a slide deck with usage metrics, outcomes delivered, and a 12-month value roadmap.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Discount and Retention Incentive Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If a customer is at renewal risk and discount is permitted by contract, offer a tiered retention incentive: (1) 5% for 1-year renewal, (2) 10% for 2-year renewal, (3) 15% for 3-year renewal plus white-glove onboarding. Never lead with discounts - first establish value, then use discount as a closing lever. Get VP approval before offering >10%.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Low Adoption Intervention Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If product adoption falls below 50%, schedule an Adoption Workshop within 14 days. Identify the top 3 unused features relevant to the customer's use case. Assign a Solutions Engineer to run a hands-on training session. Follow up with a 30-day adoption improvement plan and weekly check-ins. Measure adoption weekly post-workshop.

scenarios.name: Strategic EBR, Adoption Workshop & Analytics Deep Dive
scenarios.summary: Execute the customer's requested Executive Business Review within 7 days, involving internal and customer executives to address overall satisfaction, ROI, and future roadmap. Simultaneously, initiate an Accelerated Adoption Program, including a hands-on workshop led by a Solutions Engineer to improve onboarding and feature usage. Integrate a detailed discussion and demo of the Analytics Module, positioning it as a key solution to their data needs and a driver for increased value and adoption.

scenarios.name: Accelerated Adoption Program & Value Validation (Pre-EBR)
scenarios.summary: Prioritize an intensive 30-day Accelerated Adoption Program, including a hands-on workshop led by a Solutions Engineer to immediately address onboarding frustrations and low feature usage. Defer the Executive Business Review until demonstrable adoption improvements can be presented, aiming to build positive momentum. Once improvements are validated, schedule the EBR to review progress, reinforce value, and discuss the Analytics Module as a further enhancement.

scenarios.name: Proactive Discounted Renewal Offer & Analytics Pitch
scenarios.summary: Immediately acknowledge customer dissatisfaction and proactively offer a tiered discount (e.g., 10-15%) for a multi-year renewal to secure the contract and mitigate immediate churn risk. Simultaneously, pitch the Analytics Module as a solution to their data visibility problems, hoping it will drive future perceived value. Postpone deeper operational reviews and adoption workshops until the renewal is secured, focusing on the financial incentive to retain the customer.

bestScenario.name: Strategic EBR, Adoption Workshop & Analytics Deep Dive
bestScenario.summary: Execute the customer's requested Executive Business Review within 7 days, involving internal and customer executives to address overall satisfaction, ROI, and future roadmap. Simultaneously, initiate an Accelerated Adoption Program, including a hands-on workshop led by a Solutions Engineer to improve onboarding and feature usage. Integrate a detailed discussion and demo of the Analytics Module, positioning it as a key solution to their data needs and a driver for increased value and adoption.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Executive Sponsor Engagement Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If no executive sponsor is identified, work with your champion to map the org chart and identify the economic buyer. Schedule a sponsor introduction call within 30 days. Send monthly executive summary emails with business outcomes, key metrics, and upcoming milestones. The executive sponsor should attend every QBR/EBR.

domainDetection.reasoning: Heuristic fallback detected multiple Customer Success signals when Gemini was unavailable.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Discount and Retention Incentive Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If a customer is at renewal risk and discount is permitted by contract, offer a tiered retention incentive: (1) 5% for 1-year renewal, (2) 10% for 2-year renewal, (3) 15% for 3-year renewal plus white-glove onboarding. Never lead with discounts - first establish value, then use discount as a closing lever. Get VP approval before offering >10%.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Expansion and Upsell Opportunity Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: Identify expansion opportunities when: (1) customer adoption is above 70%, (2) they have expressed interest in additional modules, (3) their team has grown. Steps: (1) Prepare a usage report showing value delivered, (2) Present product roadmap with upcoming features, (3) Propose expansion SKUs aligned with their pain points, (4) Involve your AE for commercial negotiation. Target: expand ARR by 20%+ on renewal.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Proactive Renewal Management Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: Initiate renewal conversations 90 days before the renewal date. If auto-renewal is disabled, treat this as a high-priority renewal risk. Steps: (1) Review contract value and discount eligibility, (2) Schedule renewal discovery call with economic buyer, (3) Prepare a Business Value Assessment showing ROI over the contract period, (4) Present renewal proposal with multi-year incentive if applicable.

explanations.reason: Retention incentives are permitted by contract.
Product adoption metrics are missing from uploaded documents.
No NPS or customer satisfaction metrics were detected.
Renewal planning activities have not been confirmed.

domainDetection.reasoning: The document discusses customer adoption rates, customer satisfaction metrics (NPS, CSAT), onboarding challenges, the risk of non-renewal, and the need for an executive review, all of which are key indicators and responsibilities within Customer Success.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. The EBR should include the customer's executive sponsor and your internal VP of Customer Success. Agenda: review ROI achieved, address top concerns, present renewal roadmap. Prepare a slide deck with usage metrics, outcomes delivered, and a 12-month value roadmap.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Low Adoption Intervention Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If product adoption falls below 50%, schedule an Adoption Workshop within 14 days. Identify the top 3 unused features relevant to the customer's use case. Assign a Solutions Engineer to run a hands-on training session. Follow up with a 30-day adoption improvement plan and weekly check-ins. Measure adoption weekly post-workshop.

knowledgeGuidance.title: Customer Health Data Collection Playbook
knowledgeGuidance.action: If adoption or NPS data is unavailable, immediately request access to product analytics dashboards. Schedule a data-sharing onboarding session. Send a CSAT survey via email within 3 days. Set up automated health score tracking in your CRM. Without health data, you are flying blind - this is a P1 task.

domainDetection.reasoning: The documents are heavily focused on customer health metrics (adoption rates, NPS, CSAT), renewal status, customer satisfaction issues (frustration with onboarding), and strategies to improve customer retention and drive expansion (executive sponsors, quarterly reviews, escalation of renewal concerns). While financial terms like ARR and contract value are present in the agreement, the overarching narrative is centered on managing the customer relationship to ensure successful outcomes and renewals, which are core functions of Customer Success.
